incidents.vector: Unspecified cyber attack on exchange infrastructure forced operations to be suspended while investigation is underway.
incidents.impact: Russia-linked crypto exchange Grinex suspended all trading and withdrawals after a cyber attack. Scope of theft or data exposure not yet disclosed.

incidents.victim: Ukrainian prosecutors, NATO allied government and military bodies
incidents.vector: Credential-theft and mailbox compromise campaign targeting Microsoft and webmail accounts of officials; scope uncovered only after attackers inadvertently exposed their staging server.
incidents.impact: More than 284 inboxes compromised (Sept 2024 – March 2026), including Ukraine's Specialized Anti-Corruption Prosecutor's Office, ARMA, the Prosecutor's Training Center, 67 Romanian Air Force accounts, 27 Hellenic National Defense General Staff accounts, and officials in Bulgaria and Serbia. Thousands of emails stolen.

incidents.vector: Attempted intrusion into operational technology (OT) systems controlling a western Swedish heat-and-power facility; attack was blocked by the facility's built-in protections.
incidents.impact: Sweden's civil defense minister disclosed that pro-Russian hackers linked to Russian intelligence tried to disrupt plant operations in spring 2025. Similar attempts seen in Norway, Denmark and Poland.

incidents.vector: Attackers used AWS credentials harvested by the upstream TeamPCP compromise of the Trivy vulnerability scanner to authenticate directly to the Commission's cloud environment.
incidents.impact: CERT-EU confirmed over 90GB of data was stolen from the European Commission's AWS account and leaked on the dark web after the group chose not to extort the victim. Investigation ongoing.

incidents.vector: Active exploitation of CVE-2026-32201, an improper-input-validation spoofing flaw in on-prem SharePoint that lets unauthenticated attackers impersonate trusted content and harvest credentials.
incidents.impact: Microsoft confirmed in-the-wild exploitation during Patch Tuesday; CISA added the zero-day to the KEV catalog. Attackers use spoofed SharePoint content to run phishing and data-manipulation campaigns at scale.

incidents.vector: A planned system update caused protected home addresses to become visible to recipients of domestic payment transactions when they opened detailed payment information.
incidents.impact: Danske Bank disclosed that roughly 20,600 clients with protected-address status had their home addresses inadvertently exposed to payment counterparties. No systems were breached but regulatory impact is likely.

incidents.vector: Extortion attempt using videos shot by two former employees that allegedly depict backend customer data; attackers demanded a ransom to suppress the footage.
incidents.impact: Kraken refused to pay, fired the two insiders, notified affected customers and engaged law enforcement. The exchange says no external customer systems were compromised.

incidents.vector: Undisclosed intrusion into a system holding customer reservation data; no financial information reported to have been stolen.
incidents.impact: Booking.com notified customers that names, emails, home addresses, phone numbers and booking details were exposed. The company reset reservation PIN numbers for affected users.

incidents.vector: Data originally stolen from Hallmark's Salesforce tenant during the 2025 Salesforce-abuse wave; dumped online after Hallmark refused to pay.
incidents.impact: ShinyHunters leaked personal information for approximately 1.7 million Hallmark customers. Hallmark says it will notify those affected; no payment data exposed.

incidents.vector: In-the-wild exploitation of CVE-2026-35616, an improper-access-control zero-day in FortiClient EMS (CVSS 9.8); exploitation observed as early as March 31.
incidents.impact: Fortinet issued an emergency hotfix. Shadowserver counted nearly 2,000 publicly exposed FortiClient EMS instances. CISA added the flaw to the KEV catalog; a full patch is still pending.

incidents.vector: Spear-phish of a developer; attackers deployed a malicious pod that exposed a highly privileged Kubernetes service-account token, pivoted to cloud APIs, and accessed backend financial systems.
incidents.impact: Unit 42 disclosed a mid-2025 intrusion — attributed to the same North Korean actor behind the Bybit and BitoPro thefts — that resulted in the theft of millions of dollars in cryptocurrency and persistent backdoors in production pods.

incidents.victim: NYK (Nippon Yusen Kaisha) marine fuel procurement
incidents.vector: Unauthorized access to NYK's bunker-fuel procurement platform at the end of March; NYK stopped using the platform once intrusion was detected.
incidents.impact: Japanese shipping giant NYK — part of the world's sixth-largest container-shipping group — said employee data was accessed and likely exfiltrated. Fuel operations temporarily disrupted.

incidents.victim: Global SOHO router users; ~200 government/military organizations
incidents.vector: GRU Unit 26165 exploited known flaws in end-of-life Mikrotik and TP-Link routers to alter DNS settings, route OAuth tokens through attacker-controlled resolvers, and perform adversary-in-the-middle attacks on Microsoft Outlook Web.
incidents.impact: At peak in Dec 2025 the espionage network spanned 18,000+ routers across 23+ US states, siphoning Microsoft Office authentication tokens from more than 200 organizations. DOJ and FBI executed 'Operation Masquerade' to remotely remediate US routers.

incidents.vector: Successful phishing attack against a staff member that granted attackers access to client document repositories.
incidents.impact: Jones Day confirmed hackers accessed and posted client materials online. The firm represents more than 2,400 lawyers and high-profile clients, elevating concern about legal privilege and litigation exposure.

incidents.victim: US energy and water critical infrastructure
incidents.vector: Scanning for and opportunistic exploitation of internet-exposed industrial control systems (PLCs/SCADA), amplified by escalating Middle East hostilities.
incidents.impact: US authorities warned that Iranian-linked actors are launching disruptive attacks against roughly 3,900 exposed ICS devices in the US energy and water sectors. Sector operators advised to harden OT systems and remove public exposure.

incidents.victim: Open-source software ecosystem (LiteLLM, Trivy, Checkmarx GitHub Actions, BuddyBoss)
incidents.vector: TeamPCP stole maintainer credentials and pushed credential-harvesting payloads into widely-used packages and CI/CD workflows, exfiltrating cloud credentials, Kubernetes tokens, SSH keys and AI API keys.
incidents.impact: Cascading compromises hit at least five software ecosystems in five days. LiteLLM (~97M monthly downloads) and Checkmarx GitHub Actions were poisoned; Trivy releases bundled malware; 250 BuddyBoss-hosted university sites compromised. Downstream victims include Mercor (valued at $10B), Cisco (source code stolen) and the European Commission.

incidents.vector: Malicious GitHub Action (tied to the TeamPCP/Trivy supply-chain compromise) harvested developer credentials and AWS keys from Cisco's build environment.
incidents.impact: Attackers exfiltrated source code from internal Cisco systems and credentials for some AWS servers, then began extortion negotiations with the company.

incidents.vector: Social engineering of Hims & Hers support staff gave attackers access to the customer support portal and ticket data.
incidents.impact: Hims & Hers disclosed that personal data shared in support tickets (primarily customer names and email addresses) was stolen in a February 2026 breach. Scope of sensitive-health-data exposure still being determined.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack against the shared Patriot Regional Emergency Communications Center disrupted police and fire dispatch for four Massachusetts towns (Ashby, Dunstable, Pepperell, Townsend).
incidents.impact: Multi-day disruption of department services and phone lines across the four municipalities. No incident attribution has been released.

incidents.vector: Operators posed as a legitimate quantitative-trading firm, built trust through conferences and a $1M vault deposit, then compromised staff via malicious Apple TestFlight apps and VS Code extensions to collect multisig approvals.
incidents.impact: Attackers drained approximately $280 million in crypto-assets from Drift's treasury. Investigators linked the heist to a North Korean state group previously tied to the Bybit theft.

incidents.vector: Undisclosed intrusion forced Hasbro to shut down affected systems and deploy intermediary infrastructure to keep orders and shipping flowing.
incidents.impact: Hasbro disclosed in an SEC 8-K that recovery will take weeks. No ransomware group has claimed responsibility yet.

incidents.vector: Social-engineered the project admin into "collaborating" on a separate repo, infected their workstation with a cookie/token/credential stealer, and used the harvested access to publish malicious Axios versions.
incidents.impact: The widely-used HTTP client for JavaScript was weaponized for credential theft at scale; downstream impact confirmed at OpenAI (Mac apps required emergency updates). Blast radius still being triaged across NPM.

incidents.vector: Qilin ransomware operators compromised party IT systems; specifics of initial access not disclosed.
incidents.impact: Die Linke shut down its IT network after ransomware execution. Employee records were stolen; the party says no member data was compromised.

incidents.vector: Breach originated at Canadian business-outsourcing provider Telus Digital and cascaded into multiple telecom customer-data environments.
incidents.impact: Rogers, Freedom Mobile and its subsidiary Fido all notified customers of data-breach impact arising from the third-party Telus Digital compromise. Full scope of exposed customer data still emerging.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack on the museum's IT systems; no passwords or security maps were reported stolen.
incidents.impact: Italy's top art museum moved House of Medici treasures (Leonardo, Raphael, Botticelli) to secure bank vaults as a precaution. Museum says the incident is "nothing like the Louvre" and no physical security data leaked.
